_source: TH_EXPLORE_IT_DATA.json (authoritative structured data). Real accessions, per-item licence + open_access flag. Nothing authored by an assistant.

station.tagline: Discipline is the only apology she's ever needed to give.

museum_objects.title: 1 Yen Coin (1870)
museum_objects.description: One of the earliest yen coins struck under Japan's New Currency Act, part of the Meiji government's modernization program.
museum_objects.significance: Aiko was born the moment Japan decided to abandon centuries of feudal currency and adopt a single, disciplined, Western-style system almost overnight.
museum_objects.museum: Smithsonian National Museum of American History
museum_objects.plain_language: One of the first yen coins ever made, from when Japan completely redesigned its money system almost overnight.
museum_objects.institution: Smithsonian National Museum of American History

resolved.via: TIER 2 — free API key required (CEO signup)

museum_objects.rights_note: CITED: the original object (rights unclear/blocked). DISPLAYED: an openly-licensed image of the same kind of object, credited to its own institution. We cite one and show the other, and we say so.

museum_objects.title: Bank of Japan Currency Museum Collection
museum_objects.description: The official currency museum of the Bank of Japan, housing the full history of Japanese money.
museum_objects.significance: Everything Aiko has ever been — coin, note, digital balance — is documented here, steps from where the modern Bank of Japan itself sits.
museum_objects.plain_language: Japan's central bank's own museum, dedicated entirely to the history of Japanese money.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Meiji Tsuho Banknote
museum_objects.description: Early Meiji-era paper currency issued before the yen's formal standardization, printed using German technology.
museum_objects.significance: Before Aiko had her disciplined, modern form, she was this — an experimental note trying to hold together a government mid-transformation.
museum_objects.plain_language: An early experimental banknote from just before Japan's currency was fully standardized into the yen.

museum_objects.title: New Currency Act Original Text (1871)
museum_objects.description: The founding legislation establishing the yen as Japan's national currency, replacing the old feudal system.
museum_objects.significance: This law is the reason Aiko exists — the moment Japan chose modern discipline over centuries of feudal currency chaos.
museum_objects.plain_language: The actual 1871 law that created the yen and replaced Japan's old, complicated feudal money system.

resolved.via: long tail — needs a per-domain licence rule

museum_objects.title: Osaka Mint Museum Historical Coin Press
museum_objects.description: An original 19th-century coin-striking press used at the newly established Osaka Mint.
museum_objects.significance: This machine physically stamped Aiko's discipline into every coin — a literal engine of the modernization Japan chose for itself.
museum_objects.plain_language: An actual antique coin-making machine used when Japan's mint first started producing yen coins.

world_map.map_description: Aiko was born from Japan's Meiji-era modernization and became one of the world's most disciplined reserve currencies, shaped by Tokyo, Osaka, and decades of trade with the West.

nodes.label: Tokyo (Bank of Japan)
nodes.description: Home of the Bank of Japan and its Currency Museum, documenting the full history of the yen.

nodes.label: Osaka Mint
nodes.description: Site of Japan's first modern mint, established in 1871 to produce standardized yen coinage.

nodes.label: Yokohama
nodes.description: Japan's key international trading port during the Meiji era, central to the yen's early foreign exchange.

nodes.label: Hong Kong
nodes.description: A major regional financial hub through which yen-denominated trade flowed across Asia.

nodes.label: Bretton Woods, New Hampshire
nodes.description: Where the yen's postwar fixed exchange rate to the dollar was established as part of the global monetary order.
